stories.title: Schwarber Seizes the MLB HR Lead — 17th Homer Ties Phillies Record of 5 Straight HR Games

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Kyle Schwarber hit his 17th home run Tuesday, passing Judge and Murakami to take the MLB lead.

He's now homered in 5 straight games — tying a Phillies franchise record held by Mike Schmidt, Chase Utley, and others.

The HR race has a new frontrunner. 💪 #Phillies #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Schwarber has hit 6 HRs in 5 games for the first time in his career.

His 17th HR gave the Phillies a 2-1 win over the Red Sox on Tuesday. The Phillies are now 11-3 under Don Mattingly since firing Rob Thomson.

Everything is clicking in Philadelphia right now. ⚾ #Phillies #MLB

article.headline: Schwarber Seizes MLB HR Lead With 17th, Ties Phillies Franchise Record of 5 Straight Homer Games

research.facts:
Schwarber HR Record / HR Race Leader
**Source:** Washington Post, SI.com, DownBeach/Breaking AC, CBS Sports
- Kyle Schwarber hit his 17th HR of the season on May 12 (HIGH)
- HR was the game-winning hit in Phillies 2-1 win over Red Sox (HIGH — ESPN final score confirmed)
- Schwarber is now the MLB HR leader (HIGH — from HR race overview, 17 ahead of Judge/Murakami ~15-16)
- Schwarber tied Phillies franchise record: HRs in 5 consecutive games (HIGH — Washington Post, SI.com)
- List of Phillies who previously held this record: Bobby Abreu, Dick Allen, Odúbel Herrera, Rhys Hoskins, Mike Schmidt, Trea Turner, Chase Utley (HIGH — SI.com confirms)
- 6 HRs in a 5-game span for Schwarber is a personal first (HIGH — WaPo)
- Phillies overall record: 20-22 (HIGH)
- Phillies record under Mattingly: 11-3 (HIGH — multiple sources)
**Confidence:** All facts HIGH.

stories.title: Braves Stifle Cubs on Combined One-Hitter, 5-2 — Holmes Anchors NL's Best Pitching Staff

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
The Braves held the Cubs to ONE hit Tuesday night.

Holmes combined with 3 relievers in a 5-2 win. Yastrzemski went 2-for-2 with a 2-run HR and 3 RBI. Austin Riley also went deep.

Atlanta (29-13) leads baseball in runs allowed per game. Built differently. ⚾ #Braves #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Cubs (27-15) have had TWO 10-game win streaks this season. First team since the 1955 Dodgers to do it in first 40 decisions.

But Tuesday in Atlanta? The Braves' pitching staff allowed 1 hit.

NL's top two teams. Series continues tonight. ⚾ #Cubs #Braves #MLB

stories.title: Mattingly's Phillies Keep Rolling — 11-3 Since Thomson Firing Has Philadelphia Believing

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
The Phillies were 9-19 when they fired Rob Thomson on April 28.

Don Mattingly took over. Since then: 11-3. Schwarber has gone nuclear. Philly believes again.

From worst record in baseball to back in the race — all in 14 games. ⚾ #Phillies #MLB

article.headline: Mattingly's Phillies Are 11-3 Since Thomson Firing — One of the Best Managerial Turnarounds in Years

stories.title: Henry Bolte Makes His MLB Debut — Oakland's Top Prospect Arrives in the Big Leagues

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Henry Bolte made his MLB debut Tuesday for the A's.

He forced the call-up with a 12-for-12 streak in the minors — yes, 12 consecutive hits with 9 extra-base hits. At Triple-A this year: .348 BA, 12 HR, 17 SB in 37 games.

Oakland's best prospect is here. 📈 #Athletics #MLB

article.headline: Henry Bolte Makes His MLB Debut — Oakland's Hottest Prospect Forced His Way to the Show

stories.title: Elly De La Cruz Is Baseball's Best Shortstop Right Now — A Mid-May Reality Check

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Elly De La Cruz: .288 BA, 10 HR, 29 RBI, 9 SB. Leads all SS in WAR.

He's the first shortstop since 1900 to hit 5+ HRs and steal 5+ bases in a team's first 16 games.

The best shortstop in baseball right now might not even be getting his due. ⚾ #Reds #MLB
